channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israelis are wary of returning to the north because they don't trust the ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Even after a tenuous Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took hold this week, the mood among residents of northern Israel is far from celebratory.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: First look at Notre Dame Cathedral's restored interior five years after devastating fire
item.description: Images and videos reveal the restored interior of the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris five years after a massive fire caused extensive damage, including a collapsed roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon workers in India join Black Friday strike action for better wages and working conditions
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Amazon staff in India have joined strike action calling for better wages and working conditions as the company prepares for one of the busiest shopping periods of the year.
item.dc:creator: SHONAL GANGULY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: On a bright winter day, workers at a Ukrainian thermal power plant repair its heavily damaged equipment as drops of water from melted snow leak through gaping holes in its battered roof.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin praises 'intelligent' Trump amid assassination threats
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin praised President-elect Trump as an “intelligent” and “experienced” leader Thursday, while noting he does not believe the president-elect is “safe” following the two attempts on his life earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Israel cease-fire deal has critics worried over Biden, Obama officials looking to stymie incoming Trump admin
item.description: After 14 months of war between Israel and Hezbollah, a cease-fire went into effect this week amid allegations that the truce happened due to pressure put on Israel despite denials from the administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 100 people are missing after a boat capsized in northern Nigeria
item.description: The boat was taking the passengers from Kogi state along the river to neighboring Niger early Friday morning when it capsized
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After more than five years of frenetic, but sometimes interrupted, reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral showed itself anew to the world Friday, with rebuilt soaring ceilings and creamy good-as-new stonework erasing somber memories of its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — Ireland is voting Friday in a parliamentary election that will decide the next government — and will show whether Ireland bucks the global trend of incumbents being ousted by disgruntled voters after years of pandemic, international instability and a cost-of-living pressures.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy ordered missile strikes into Russia hours after trip to front lines with Fox News
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy recently discussed the latest developments with his country's war against Russia with Fox News chief foreign correspondent Trey Yingst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chad ends a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Chad's government is ending a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler, to redefine the nation's sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK intelligence chief accuses Russia of 'staggeringly reckless' sabotage campaign
item.description: The head of Britain’s foreign intelligence service says Russia is conducting a “staggeringly reckless” sabotage campaign against Ukraine's allies that risks dangerously escalating the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senior figure with Scholz's ex-allies quits over 'D-Day' paper on German government collapse
item.description: A senior figure in the party whose departure from Chancellor Olaf Scholz’s coalition three weeks ago put Germany on the road to an early election has resigned in a furor over an internal document whose title referred to “D-Day.”.
item.dc:creator: Geir Moulson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: High-ranking military official in China suspended and placed under investigation
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A member of China's powerful Central Military Commission has been suspended and put under investigation, the country's Defense Ministry said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DAKE KANG and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian insurgents launch large-scale attack on government forces in the country's northwest
item.description: Syrian armed groups launched a large-scale attack on areas controlled by government forces and seized territory in northwestern Syria, opposition groups said Thursday. The government and its allies responded with airstrikes and shelling to ward off the insurgent advances, activists said.
item.dc:creator: BEIRUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin mulls striking Kyiv with new hypersonic missile that can reportedly reach US West Coast
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says that Ukraine’s "decision-making centers" in Kyiv could be targeted next using a new hypersonic missile that could also potentially reach the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Space Force looks to bulk up against anti-satellite weapons
item.description: The 5-year-old U.S. Space Force is moving quickly to confront what is becoming its priority challenge: the threat of anti-satellite weapons (ASATs) from foreign adversaries, including Russia and China.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah militants returned home Wednesday as a ceasefire took hold, driving cars stacked with personal belongings and defying warnings from Lebanese and Israeli troops to avoid some areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he had a 'very productive conversation' with Mexico’s president
item.description: President-elect Trump said he had a “very productive” conversation with Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum. “Just had a wonderful conversation with the new President of Mexico, Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo,” Trump posted Wednesday to his Truth Social site.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US sanctions 21 more Maduro allies accused of post-election repression in Venezuela
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on 21 more allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro for repressing Venezuelans following the country's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian police officer who used Taser on 95-year-old woman found guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 'wrongfully detained' Americans, White House says
item.description: China is releasing three Americans who were "wrongfully detained," a National Security Council spokesperson says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
